pi_items.source: Task description and verifier.py logic explicitly require rule ID 100300.
Task description and verifier.py logic explicitly require rule level 10.
setup_task.sh script explicitly writes the synthetic JSON logs to this path.
export_result.sh and verifier.py explicitly target this file for extracting and evaluating the custom rule.
verifier.py uses these exact strings from the metadata to test the user's regex, which match the task instructions for checking `-EncodedCommand` flags.

While not directly evaluated in verifier.py, these are correct logical non-matches as per the task description's scope to only target Base64 encoded commands. The provided benign sample also uses `-ExecutionPolicy Bypass`.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated Windows Event logs injected via setup_task.sh. The expected custom Wazuh rule ID is 100300 at level 10, and must be created in /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml. The rule must successfully match PowerShell commands utilizing various case-insensitive truncations of the `-EncodedCommand` flag.
